title_normalized: Senior Software Engineer, AI Developer Tools

architecture.scale_signals: 20 million monthly users; 20 billion image pulls

success_metrics: accelerate developer productivity
build and ship production-ready AI agents and tools
enable teams across Docker to rapidly build and deploy their own AI developer tools
transform AI developer tools into commercial offerings
maintain high quality bars
implied_pain_points: developers need AI tools to speed up code review, test generation, deployment diagnostics, and on-call assistance
Docker needs a self-service platform for internal teams to create and deploy AI developer tools quickly
AI systems must be production-ready, reliable, and cost-controlled

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A top CV needs evidence of shipping production LLM or agent systems, not just prototypes, and should show ownership from design through operations. It should also demonstrate prompt engineering, evaluation, cost/rate control, and platform thinking for reusable AI tooling.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Built AI developer tools specifically for code review, test generation, deployment diagnostics, or on-call assistance if that was not done
Led adoption of Claude Code, Cursor, or Warp across an engineering organization unless explicitly proven
Owned Docker-scale internal AI tooling infrastructure without direct evidence of that scale
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4: enterprise AI workflow platform with 42 plugins and 2,000 users; strongest proof of production LLM/agent delivery, orchestration, and evaluation
Lantern: LLM gateway with multi-provider routing and circuit breaking; matches LLM integration, cost/performance control, and resilience
Guardrails, semantic caching, and operational ownership; maps well to the self-service AI developer tools platform

architecture.scale_signals: 3.5 million SMB customers; 60+ brands across 22 European countries

success_metrics: unlock the potential of GenAI across the team.blue group
design scalable AI powered software solutions
ensure seamless operation and performance optimization
test and deploy software solutions that meet business needs
deliver innovative AI solutions
implied_pain_points: team.blue needs to introduce GenAI across many existing brands and workflows
AI features must integrate into current environments without disrupting operations
RAG and agentic systems need to work reliably in production for SMB-facing products

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should show production RAG or agentic systems built in Python, integrated into existing products or workflows rather than isolated demos. It should also show experience with vector search, OpenAI-style APIs, and cross-functional delivery in a product environment.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Fine-tuned foundation models in production unless that work actually happened
Built MCP server-based agent systems if there is no direct evidence
Scaled OpenSearch or FAISS-backed retrieval systems for customer-facing production use unless proven
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4: RAG, evaluation, guardrails, and enterprise workflow automation; strongest match for scalable AI products and production RAG
Lantern: multi-provider routing and circuit breaker; useful for AI API integration and reliability
CQRS/event-sourcing and microservices background; shows backend product integration into existing environments

hard_skills_nice_to_have: multi-agent architectures for analytics or automation
success_metrics: design and deploy agentic systems and AI pipelines
ensure high-performance deployments of LLMs in production environments
build scalable production-grade LLM pipelines
guide architectural decisions
implied_pain_points: the company needs real-world agentic AI applications and multi-agent orchestration
LLM deployments must be production-grade and high-performing
architectural decisions need to be guided by a hands-on lead working directly with the CTO

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A top CV should show hands-on agentic system design, open-source LLM deployment, and practical use of LangChain or LangGraph in production. It should also demonstrate architectural leadership, vector search, and operating model-serving stacks rather than only API usage.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Experience deploying with vLLM or TGI if that was not actually done
Hands-on production work with Qwen, Llama, or Mistral if those models were not used
Direct people management or formal CTO-level reporting responsibilities if unsupported
GDPR compliance ownership beyond what is explicitly proven
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4: multi-plugin agent platform with orchestration and evaluation; closest match to agentic systems and production LLM pipelines
Lantern: multi-provider routing and circuit breaker; useful for production deployment and reliability thinking
Engineering leader / software architect background; supports the architectural decision-making and hands-on leadership expectation

success_metrics: enhance vulnerability discovery
improve security analysis workflows
expand how thousands of customers detect and respond to emerging threats
identify risks in LLMs and applied AI systems
implied_pain_points: build safer AI-powered security workflows into the platform
operationalize agentic AI for security use cases
add memory, orchestration, and long-horizon reasoning to Hai
support AI red teaming and model risk evaluation

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show shipped AI platform work, not just prototypes. It should prove experience building agentic or LLM-powered systems with clear evidence of secure workflows, evaluation, and production deployment at meaningful customer scale.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: led a security research org
owned budget or P&L
managed direct reports
built AI red teaming systems without clear evidence
shipped security-specific AI workflows at enterprise scale without explicit proof
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps well because it shows enterprise AI workflow platform experience with RAG, evaluation, and guardrails.
Lantern maps well because it demonstrates multi-provider routing and circuit-breaker style reliability for LLM systems.
The candidate's software architect and technical lead background fits the platform-oriented, cross-functional nature of the role.

architecture.latency_reliability: scalability, performance, and reliability of AI systems

success_metrics: design and implement AI-powered features for Stella
build production-ready systems that integrate LLMs with platform functionality
process and structure knowledge base content for AI consumption
develop context-aware AI capabilities that understand user workflows
ensure scalability, performance, and reliability of AI systems
implied_pain_points: turn LLM ideas into production product features
make knowledge base content usable by AI systems
build context-aware assistants that understand user workflows
keep AI systems scalable and reliable in production

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show production LLM feature work, not just experimentation. It should prove the candidate can build retrieval, context management, and agentic workflows that survive real user traffic and operational constraints.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: deep JavaScript/TypeScript production ownership if the evidence is thin
expertise in tool-calling frameworks without a concrete implementation
ownership of serverless edge architectures without explicit project proof
formal team management experience
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps strongly because it shows enterprise AI workflow delivery, RAG, evaluation, semantic caching, and guardrails.
Lantern maps strongly because it shows multi-provider LLM infrastructure and routing reliability.
The candidate's architect-level experience maps well to the role's need for production AI systems and platform integration.

success_metrics: bridge experimental notebooks and production-grade AI services
deploy, monitor, and scale bespoke models and LLMs
design and implement RAG architectures
own the data lifecycle within Google Cloud Platform
integrate Salesforce Data Cloud with internal AI engines to drive hyper-personalized user experiences
implied_pain_points: move AI work from experimentation into production
create real-time, high-quality context for LLMs from proprietary data
operationalize AI data pipelines on GCP
connect CRM data with AI systems for personalization

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show production data and AI platform work on a cloud stack, especially where data pipelines feed LLM systems. It should prove hands-on ownership of scalable architecture, retrieval pipelines, and model serving or orchestration in a real business setting.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: deep GCP ownership if the evidence is limited to adjacent cloud experience
hands-on Vertex AI or BigQuery expertise without direct project proof
Salesforce Data Cloud integration experience without explicit evidence
formal ownership of a data engineering org
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps well because it shows production AI workflow delivery, RAG, and data-driven orchestration.
Lantern maps well because it shows infrastructure for routing and controlling LLM access in production.
The candidate's backend and microservices background supports the operational and integration aspects of the role, though the GCP-specific parts must be evidenced carefully.

architecture.scale_signals: Supports more than 28,000 organizations; the broader Sophos portfolio defends more than 600,000 organizations worldwide.

success_metrics: model accuracy
model comparison
engineering metrics
traceability
reproducibility
cost-efficient deliverables
implied_pain_points: Need to automate and maintain ML operations and training pipelines.
Need to improve monitoring, QA, and reproducibility of retrained models.
Need to integrate models into multiple products reliably at scale.
Need to keep AI delivery high quality while controlling costs.
Need to coordinate work across global teams.

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should show ownership of an AI or ML platform, especially where Python services, SQL-backed systems, and automated pipelines were built and maintained in AWS. It should also show measurable improvements in model quality, monitoring, reproducibility, or delivery efficiency across a cross-functional environment.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Full ownership of an MLOps stack unless the CV shows training pipeline automation and model monitoring work
Deep hands-on experience with AWS Fargate, CodeBuild, Batch, or SageMaker unless those services are explicitly on the CV
Snowflake production experience if the candidate only shows generic SQL or other databases
C or C++ expertise if that work is not explicitly demonstrated
Formal model benchmarking or ML evaluation ownership if the CV only shows application-level AI features
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the strongest match because it is an enterprise AI workflow platform with RAG, evaluation, guardrails, semantic caching, and 2,000 users, which aligns with ML operations, quality, and product integration.
Lantern is relevant for dependable AI infrastructure patterns such as multi-provider routing and circuit breaking, which supports the reliability angle of the role.
Earlier backend and microservices work maps to the Python, SQL, maintainable-code, and service-delivery expectations, but it is secondary to the AI platform experience.
